title: GCP project IAM policy binding
description: One record per (role, member) binding in a GCP project's IAM policy, enriched with service-account inventory facts (whether the member is a service account and whether that account is disabled). Pulled read-only from Cloud Resource Manager (getIamPolicy) + the IAM service-accounts list (roles/iam.securityReviewer). Binding + assignment METADATA only — never a service-account KEY, never object/secret contents. The platform evaluator decides least-privilege fit per (control, binding); the connector emits descriptive (INCONCLUSIVE) records, except a disabled service-account grant which is PASS (inert).

member.description: GCP IAM member string (e.g. serviceAccount:svc@proj.iam.gserviceaccount.com, user:alice@example.com, group:..). The opaque principal identifier GCP itself records on the binding — not personal contact PII.

role.description: GCP IAM role on the binding (e.g. roles/owner, roles/storage.admin, roles/viewer).

is_privileged.description: Connector-side heuristic: the role is a known high-privilege / primitive role (owner/editor/IAM-admin family).

is_service_acc.description: True when the bound member is a service account (vs a human/group).

disabled.description: For service-account members: the service account is disabled (deprovisioned) — the grant is inert.

reason.description: Human-readable reason when the verdict is pass/inconclusive.
